headline: 2.5 Million Workers With Zero Safety Net
context: DOSM estimates 2.5 million gig and informal workers lack EPF, SOCSO, or EIS coverage, while Parliament debates a voluntary opt-in framework.

cards.big: No EPF. No SOCSO. No sick leave. No floor.
cards.sub: Grab drivers, Foodpanda riders, Shopee freelancers — 2.5 million people outside the system.

cards.big: Voluntary opt-in schemes achieve 8–12% uptake globally.
cards.sub: Malaysia's i-Saraan voluntary EPF programme: 6% take-up after five years.

cards.big: Platform companies are not legally employers under the Employment Act.
cards.sub: The 2022 amendment excluded app-based engagement from the employer definition.

cards.big: One workplace injury costs the average rider 14 months of income.
cards.sub: Without SOCSO, medical costs come from savings that do not exist.

cards.big: Voluntary protection for involuntary risk is not protection.
cards.sub: The worker cannot opt out of injury. Why can the platform opt out of coverage?

cards.big: Like building a house with no insurance — everything works until the first storm, and then you lose everything
cards.sub: Voluntary opt-in protection means the most vulnerable workers are the least likely to enroll

cards.big: The Employment Act was written for factories. The economy moved. The law did not.
